claim_level: contract audit only; no native MuJoCo PPO/VAE/diffusion rollout claim
hard_blockers: native_action_adapter_rollout_ready is false because MuJoCo ctrlrange still clips legal unit actions
current MuJoCo PPO probe is explicitly approximate, not an official observation-manager match
empirical_normalization must be preserved for any direct actor checkpoint inference
dimension-correct 160-D observation is not sufficient evidence of semantic correctness

interpretation.why_current_videos_can_fail_even_if_acto: The actor was trained on normalized IsaacLab observations with exact command/frame semantics. A MuJoCo-side approximate 160-D vector can be dimension-correct but semantically wrong, producing front-leaning or collapsed postures instead of lifted-leg/walk control.

paper_and_official_contract.motion_reanchoring: MotionCommand increments continuous time_steps, resets via adaptive sampling, then aligns desired body poses by robot anchor x/y, reference anchor z, and yaw_quat(robot_anchor * inv(reference_anchor)).

required_next_steps: Export an official motion policy ONNX with metadata and embedded normalizer, or load the checkpoint obs normalizer exactly.
Implement a native MuJoCo observation builder that returns the exact eight policy terms and slices in this audit.
Validate that builder numerically against IsaacLab observation_manager output for the same reset state, motion time_step, and last_action.
Validate frame-alignment semantics against motion_tracking_controller worldToInit_/Pinocchio local-frame formulas.
Validate body-frame base velocity, Rot6D column ordering, default_joint_pos source, and previous-action semantics with finite numeric fixtures.
Resolve or justify MuJoCo ctrlrange clipping in the native action adapter before allowing no-root-assist policy videos.
Combine the validated obs builder with the native action adapter fixture, disable root assist, and log raw/clipped normalized actions plus PD setpoints.
Only after the above gates pass should a native MuJoCo PPO rollout video be treated as motion-control evidence.
scope: Audits the exact 160-D IsaacLab policy observation contract, the official/deployment frame semantics, and the empirical-normalizer requirement before using IsaacLab PPO checkpoints in native MuJoCo.

term_rows.failure_mode: wrong phase/time-step or reset-spliced commands can make a good policy chase discontinuous targets
term_rows.mujoco_source_needed: reference motion joint_pos and joint_vel at the same continuous time_step
term_rows.notes: Requires the exact motion file, joint order, phase/time-step handling, and no reset-spliced time jumps.
term_rows.required_validation: compare exact 58-D command slice against IsaacLab observation_manager for the same motion file and time_step

term_rows.failure_mode: meter-scale fake anchor error can drive the actor into a persistent leaning/recovery posture
term_rows.mujoco_source_needed: robot anchor pose and reference anchor pose after official yaw/translation alignment
term_rows.notes: This is the most failure-prone term.  Wrong world-to-init or yaw alignment makes the actor see meter-scale fake error.
term_rows.required_validation: match IsaacLab subtract_frame_transforms output after MotionCommand yaw-only re-anchoring

term_rows.failure_mode: rot6D column/order mismatch can make the actor believe the target is rotated even while standing
term_rows.mujoco_source_needed: robot anchor quaternion and aligned reference anchor quaternion converted to rot6D
term_rows.notes: Rot6D order must match IsaacLab matrix_from_quat(...)[..., :2].reshape.
term_rows.official_semantics: relative anchor orientation via subtract_frame_transforms, then first two rotation-matrix columns
term_rows.required_validation: match IsaacLab matrix_from_quat(...)[..., :2].reshape ordering and quaternion convention

term_rows.failure_mode: world/body-frame velocity mismatch looks like a constant disturbance and biases recovery actions
term_rows.mujoco_source_needed: robot base/root linear velocity in the same body/local frame as IsaacLab mdp.base_lin_vel
term_rows.notes: Using world-frame qvel directly is wrong; body-frame conversion and root/body choice must be verified.
term_rows.required_validation: match IsaacLab root_lin_vel_b for the same MuJoCo/IsaacLab state, not world-frame qvel

term_rows.failure_mode: angular velocity convention mismatch can suppress leg-lift behavior and over-trigger torso stabilization
term_rows.mujoco_source_needed: robot base/root angular velocity in the same body/local frame as IsaacLab mdp.base_ang_vel
term_rows.notes: MuJoCo qvel angular convention must be checked against IsaacLab root_ang_vel_b.
term_rows.required_validation: match IsaacLab root_ang_vel_b and MuJoCo quaternion/qvel angular convention

term_rows.failure_mode: default-pose mismatch shifts every policy input and can make neutral standing look like crouching/leaning
term_rows.mujoco_source_needed: current 29 joint positions minus the exact IsaacLab default_joint_pos used by the policy
term_rows.notes: Deployment default and IsaacLab nominal default differ at ankle pitch by about 0.033 rad; prefer ONNX metadata/default_joint_pos.
term_rows.required_validation: verify default_joint_pos from exported metadata or IsaacLab ArticulationData, then compare 29-D slice

term_rows.failure_mode: joint-order or qvel-index mismatch corrupts feedback even if the action order is correct
term_rows.mujoco_source_needed: current 29 joint velocities in official joint order
term_rows.notes: Requires MuJoCo qvel order to match official action/joint rows exactly.
term_rows.required_validation: compare MuJoCo qvel joint slice against IsaacLab joint_vel_rel in official joint order

term_rows.failure_mode: feeding teacher or reference actions as last_action contaminates VAE/diffusion closed-loop observations
term_rows.mujoco_source_needed: previous clipped normalized policy action actually applied to PD setpoints
term_rows.notes: Using teacher action as last_action for VAE/diffusion variants contaminates downstream observations.
term_rows.required_validation: prove last_action is the previous normalized action applied by the same controller variant
